title: ApplicationFolders Object
description: The ApplicationFolders object provides access to the paths of various folders associated with Fusion.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

properties.name: appLogFilePath
properties.description: Returns the full filename for the current application log file.

properties.name: appStoreInstallPath
properties.description: Returns the path where apps from the Autodesk App Store are installed.

properties.name: defaultPathForScriptsAndAddIns
properties.description: Gets and sets the default path for scripts and add-ins. This is the same as the defaultPathForScriptsAndAddIns property on the APIPreferences object.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: materialsPath
properties.description: Returns the path where user-created material and appearance libraries are saved.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: optionsPath
properties.description: Returns the path to the user-specific folder where Fusion saves various options.

properties.name: rootPath
properties.description: Returns the path to the version-specific folder where Fusion is installed.

properties.name: userDataPath
properties.description: Returns the path where some user-specific data is stored.

appLogFilePath.title: ApplicationFolders.appLogFilePath Property
appLogFilePath.description: Returns the full filename for the current application log file.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = applicationFolders_var.appLogFilePath

appStoreInstallPath.title: ApplicationFolders.appStoreInstallPath Property
appStoreInstallPath.description: Returns the path where apps from the Autodesk App Store are installed.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = applicationFolders_var.appStoreInstallPath

defaultPathForScriptsAndAddIns.title: ApplicationFolders.defaultPathForScriptsAndAddIns Property
defaultPathForScriptsAndAddIns.description: Gets and sets the default path for scripts and add-ins. This is the same as the defaultPathForScriptsAndAddIns property on the APIPreferences object.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = applicationFolders_var.defaultPathForScriptsAndAddIns

# Set the value of the property.
applicationFolders_var.defaultPathForScriptsAndAddIns = propertyValue

isValid.title: ApplicationFolders.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = applicationFolders_var.isValid

materialsPath.description: Returns the path where user-created material and appearance libraries are saved.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = applicationFolders_var.materialsPath

It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:

optionsPath.description: Returns the path to the user-specific folder where Fusion saves various options.

syntax.python:

rootPath.description: Returns the path to the version-specific folder where Fusion is installed.

syntax.python:

userDataPath.title: ApplicationFolders.userDataPath Property
userDataPath.description: Returns the path where some user-specific data is stored.

syntax.python:

classType.title: ApplicationFolders.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.
